name: Renjna's Dal Soup
recipeInstructions:
1. Sort through the lentils and wash them until the water runs clear.
2. Place the lentils in a soup pot and add enough water to cover by about 2 inches. Add turmeric and 1 tablespoon of salt, then bring to a boil.
3. Reduce heat and simmer for approximately 30 minutes, or until the lentils are soft and starting to break apart. If needed, add more water to achieve desired consistency.
4. In a frying pan, heat oil over medium heat. Finely mince the garlic and coarsely chop the jalapeno peppers (remove seeds if a less spicy dish is preferred). Add garlic and peppers along with cumin seeds, garam masala, and 1/2 teaspoon of salt to the hot oil. Sauté for several minutes until garlic begins to brown.
5. Mix the sautéed spices into the lentils. Stir well; you may need to return some of the cooked dal to the pan to extract all the flavors.
6. Continue simmering for another 20 minutes or so, allowing flavors to meld together. Adjust salt as needed.
7. Garnish with chopped cilantro if desired and serve hot alongside bread or incorporate into a full Indian meal.
